name: BMP280 Pressure Sensor
tagline: Measures barometric pressure and temperature over I2C or SPI — ideal for altitude and weather sensing
description: The BMP280 is a high-precision barometric pressure and temperature sensor from Bosch. It measures absolute pressure (300–1100 hPa) and can calculate altitude based on pressure differences. It communicates over I2C or SPI. Widely used in weather stations, drone altitude control, GPS enhancement, and indoor navigation applications.

specs.operatingVoltage: 1.71V – 3.6V (use 3.3V; most modules have onboard regulator for 5V input)

pinout.name: VCC
pinout.desc: Power supply — 3.3V or 5V (if module has regulator)

pinout.name: I2C Data
pinout.desc: I2C SDA — connect to Arduino A4

pinout.name: I2C Clock
pinout.desc: I2C SCL — connect to Arduino A5

pinout.name: Chip Select
pinout.desc: Connect to VCC for I2C mode (pull HIGH)

pinout.name: I2C Address
pinout.desc: Pull LOW for 0x76, pull HIGH for 0x77

wiringNote: In I2C mode, connect CSB to VCC and set SDO to LOW or HIGH to select the I2C address. Do not leave SDO floating — it causes unstable address behavior.
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'Adafruit BMP280' → Install. Also install 'Adafruit Unified Sensor' when prompted.
commonMistakes: Wrong I2C address (0x76 vs 0x77) → 'BMP280 not found' error; scan with I2C scanner
Not installing 'Adafruit Unified Sensor' dependency → library won't compile
Leaving CSB floating in I2C mode → switches randomly between I2C and SPI
Using incorrect sea-level pressure in readAltitude() → altitude readings are off
